api: DefiLlama public API (api.llama.fi, coins.llama.fi, stablecoins.llama.fi, yields.llama.fi)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, cross-checked against DefiLlama's own published OpenAPI spec (https://github.com/DefiLlama/api-docs, defillama-openapi-free.json, fetched 2026-07-04) which enumerates the ENTIRE free-tier surface across all 4 hosts (api.llama.fi TVL/volumes/fees/perps, coins.llama.fi token prices/blocks, stablecoins.llama.fi, yields.llama.fi) as exactly 31 documented endpoints. 9 are now covered_by a stream (protocols, chains, stablecoins, dexs, fees, options, open_interest, pools, stablecoin_chains, historical_chain_tvl — 10 streams total, historical_chain_tvl and stablecoin_chains/options/open_interest/pools newly added this pass). The remaining 21 endpoints are single-identifier detail/history lookups (per-protocol, per-coin, per-pool, per-chain, per-timestamp) requiring a caller-supplied identifier this connector has no config surface for, or would require an impractical high-volume fan-out (see per-entry reasons below); a public, documented 'bridges' API (bridges.llama.fi) that shipped in DefiLlama's legacy docs is no longer present in the current OpenAPI spec at all and is not implemented (nothing to cover — not a scoping choice). DefiLlama's public API remains unauthenticated and read-only end to end; there is no write/mutation surface of any kind (capabilities.write stays false).

excluded.reason: per-protocol full historical TVL+token-breakdown detail; a single real response (e.g. binance-cex) is ~33MB of embedded daily time-series across chains/tokens for one of 7778 protocols — fanning this out over every protocols-stream id would mean 7778 multi-megabyte requests per sync, an impractical read shape this connector does not implement; the current-TVL summary fields for every protocol are already covered by the protocols stream

per-chain historical TVL time series; would require one request per chain (fanned out from the chains stream) purely to retrieve a parallel history series, not a syncable object list on its own — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single scalar current-TVL number for one protocol; the identical value is already present as the tvl field on that protocol's record in the protocols stream

requires a caller-supplied comma-separated list of chain:address token identifiers; this connector's spec.json declares no such config surface and DefiLlama publishes no enumerable catalog of valid coin identifiers to fan this out from
same caller-supplied coins-list identifier requirement as prices/current, plus a caller-chosen timestamp; no enumerable id source
requires a caller-supplied JSON map of coins to timestamp arrays; no enumerable id source and no declarative way to express an arbitrary nested batch request body as a GET query
requires a caller-supplied coins identifier list with no enumerable catalog to source ids from
requires a caller-supplied coins identifier list with no enumerable catalog to source ids from
requires a caller-supplied coins identifier list with no enumerable catalog to source ids from
single nearest-block-to-timestamp lookup; a point utility query, not a syncable object stream

all-chains-summed historical stablecoin market-cap time series; the current-snapshot circulating totals per stablecoin are already covered by the stablecoins stream, and this is a parallel time-series cut of the same underlying data, not a distinct object catalog

per-chain historical stablecoin market-cap time series; would require fanning out over every chain purely for a parallel history series, not a syncable object list on its own — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage

per-stablecoin full historical circulating-supply-by-chain detail; would require fanning out over every id in the stablecoins stream purely to retrieve a parallel history series, not a distinct current-state object — Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
historical time series of the underlying peg-reference prices (e.g. USD, EUR) stablecoins are pegged to, not a per-stablecoin or per-chain object catalog

per-pool historical APY/TVL time series; would require fanning out over every id in the pools stream (15000+) purely to retrieve a parallel history series, not a distinct current-state object — impractical fan-out volume, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
the same dexs overview records filtered to one chain; the unfiltered dexs stream already carries every protocol's chains array, so no additional record shape or coverage is gained
single-protocol dex volume detail with historical breakdown; the current summary fields are already present on that protocol's record in the dexs stream
the same options overview records filtered to one chain; the unfiltered options stream already carries every protocol's chains array
single-protocol options volume detail with historical breakdown; the current summary fields are already present on that protocol's record in the options stream
the same fees overview records filtered to one chain; the unfiltered fees stream already carries every protocol's chains array
single-protocol fees/revenue detail with historical breakdown; the current summary fields are already present on that protocol's record in the fees stream

bridges.llama.fi's public bridge-volume API previously documented by DefiLlama is no longer present in the current published OpenAPI surface (defillama-openapi-free.json, 2026-07-04) at all; treated as withdrawn/deprecated rather than a live endpoint this connector chose to skip
